mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.11%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, mechanically supporting elevated equity valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as rates stay near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX shows no positive drift bias while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.5 percentage points over two consecutive monthly releases signals accelerating labor market repair after the COVID shock, which raises consensus expectations for consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp over 2 consecutive monthly releases
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 14.7% peak to 5.7% by asof=t-1mo
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE decline trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in more than 50% of triggered instances across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect resumed factory utilization and supply-chain normalization after the pandemic shutdown, providing a leading signal of near-term corporate revenue improvement that lifts S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and next 5-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that is not detectable from either series alone; this combined signal of durable and non-durable demand strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 1% AND HOUST MoM growth > 3% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1089k units, RSAFS at 439,771M as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fiscal stimulus support
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST growth triggers fire jointly, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS is near 0% and CPIAUCSL growth remains subdued, investors face a persistent search-for-yield dynamic that channels capital into equities as bond substitutes, producing an elevated positive S&P 500 return bias over a full quarter (60 trading days) that would not hold if rates were meaningfully positive.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% for 3+ months, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive relative to the full-sample unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct ZLB episode.

mechanism_narrative: If month-over-month PAYEMS growth decelerates sharply (falling below the prior 3-month average pace by more than 50%) after the initial post-COVID rebound, it signals a stalling labor market recovery, prompting downward revisions to growth expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: MoM PAYEMS growth falls below 50% of trailing 3-month average pace
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 140,849k as of asof=t-1mo, still well below pre-COVID level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the PAYEMS deceleration trigger fires, invalidating the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: During a sharp post-shock GDP rebound regime, a positive quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 following a severe prior contraction signals the start of a V-shaped recovery cycle, which supports elevated S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days) via improving earnings and risk appetite; this relationship is regime-specific to recovery phases rather than mid-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,311.6B as of asof=t-4mo reflecting sharp Q2 2020 contraction
failure_condition: In quarters following a GDP contraction of similar magnitude, subsequent 60-day SPX returns after the first positive GDPC1 print are not significantly higher than in mid-cycle expansion baselines (p > 0.05), or the effect fails to replicate in a second historical recovery episode.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero FEDFUNDS and a declining UNRATE trend reflects a coordinated monetary-labor 'Fed put' recovery dynamic in which accommodative policy amplifies the equity market response to labor market improvement beyond what either signal predicts alone, producing stronger positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND UNRATE declining for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary easing combined with post-shock labor recovery
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trigger produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from the UNRATE-only signal (H_002) or the FEDFUNDS-only signal (H_001), indicating no genuine interaction effect (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).
